comments.body: Please clarify your specific problem or provide additional details to highlight exactly what you need. As it's currently written, it's hard to tell exactly what you're asking.

answers.body:
1. Set the universal color theme to one of the universal versions, not any specially downloaded color themes. It has to be a built-in, default IntelliJ theme (either light or dark).

    Note: this will not work if you already replaced the default color themes with any of the after-market/custom downloaded ones, because those will override the settings for each panel.

  2. Go to Settings -> Editor -> Color Theme -> Console Colors and here, in the right-side menu, you will see the Background, Input, Output, etc. Click on them and they can be changed individually.

title: How to change Run window background color?
body:
How to modify the background & foreground color only for the Run window section lower screen (where you see the code results)?

In Eclipse, that would be the console, but I can't find it in IntelliJ IDEA's latest version.
